name: Herbed Split Pea Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Fresh chives, snipped, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch the bacon in boiling water for 1 minute, drain, pat dry on paper towel, and coarsely chop.
2. Place the chopped bacon in a large soup kettle and cook over medium heat until it turns a light golden color.
3. Add the chopped onion and stir-cook for 1 minute.
4. Add the diced carrot and stir-cook for 30 seconds.
5. Add the chopped celery and stir-cook for 30 seconds.
6. Stir in the minced parsley, thyme, bay leaf, dried rosemary, split peas, Worcestershire sauce, and chicken broth.
7. Bring the soup to a boil, skimming the top as necessary.
8. Boil for 1 minute, then cover and simmer until the peas are tender, about 1 1/2 hours.
9. For serving, season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.
10. Ladle into warm bowls and top each serving with snipped chives.
11. Makes 4 generous servings.
